title: Berliner Dom - Quick Summary
items: **The Berliner Dom is Berlin’s largest Protestant church,** but its real story is survival through empire, war, GDR reconstruction and reunified Berlin.
**The current cathedral was inaugurated on 27 February 1905** after the older Dom was demolished in 1893 for Kaiser Wilhelm II’s imperial project.
**A bomb hit the dome lantern on 24 May 1944,** causing fire, collapse and severe damage to the church and Hohenzollern Crypt.
**The cathedral today is not the full 1905 building:** the northern Memorial Church was demolished during GDR-era reconstruction in 1975.
**In 2026, the Hohenzollern Crypt is open again,** and the standard visit includes the church, crypt, museum, audio guide and 50-metre dome view.
